ocr.text: In the analysis of cybersecurity incidents from 2019 to 2024, data reveals significant trends across various incident types. In
2019, there were 5,000 phishing incidents, resulting in a total cost of ¥1.2B, with an average loss of ¥240K and a response time
of 3 days. The following year, 2020, recorded 4,500 malware incidents, costing ¥1.5B, with an average loss of ¥333K and a
response time of 4 days. The trend continued in 2021 with 6,000 ransomware incidents, totaling ¥2.0B in costs, an average loss
of ¥333K, and a response time of 5 days.
Year  IncidentType  IncidentCounts  TotalCost  AverageLoss  ResponseTime
2019  Phishing  5000  ¥1.2B  ¥240K  3days
2020  Malware  4500  ¥1.5B  ¥333K  4days  2021  Ransomware
6000  ¥2.0B  ¥333K  5days  2022  DataBreach  7000  ¥2.5B
¥357K  6days  2023  DDoSAttack  3000  ¥800M  ¥267K  2days
2024  InsiderThreat  4000  ¥1.0B  ¥250K  4days  Total  30000
2020  Malware  15%  ¥150M  85%  2021  Ransomware  20%
DDoSAttack  5%  ¥40M  70%  2024  InsiderThreat  10%  ¥100M
75%  Average  15%  ¥207M  85%  Trend  Increasing  ¥1.5B
Stable  Increasing  ¥300M  Decreasing  Stable  ¥150M  Increasing  ¥1.0B
In 2022, data breaches surged to 7,000 incidents, incurring a total cost of ¥2.5B, with an average loss of ¥357K and a response
time of 6 days. In 2023, there were 3,000 DDoS attacks, costing ¥800M, with an average loss of ¥267K and a response time of
2 days. Lastly, 2024 saw 4,000 insider threats, leading to a total cost of ¥1.0B, an average loss of ¥250K, and a response time of
4 days. Cumulatively, from 2019 to 2024, the total incident count reached 30,000, with an overall cost of ¥7.0B and an average
loss of ¥233K, accompanied by an average response time of 4 days. Notably, the average loss per incident trend indicates an
increasing pattern, with total costs projected at ¥1.5B by 2024. The overall trend reflects a stable yet increasing frequency of
incidents, highlighting the critical need for enhanced cybersecurity measures in the Asia-Pacific region.
